references: EDB Mathematics Education Key Learning Area Curriculum Guide (Primary 1-6)
EDB primary Mathematics Key Stage 1 learning objectives
Public Hong Kong Primary 1 Mathematics syllabus topic examples (numbers to 20, addition and subtraction, shapes, measurement, time, money, simple data handling)
source.note: No fixed official Hong Kong P1 mathematics vocabulary list is prescribed; this list is theme-based for Primary 1 mathematics enrichment import. Term-only entries are completed during import.

words.definition: To say numbers in order to find out how many there are.
To put two or more numbers together to get a total.
words.example: If I add 2 and 3, I get 5.
words.example_translation: 如果我將2和3相加，我會得到5。

words.definition: To take one number away from another.
Being the same in amount or value.
A larger amount of something.
words.example: I have more stickers than you.

words.definition: A smaller amount of something.
The form of an object, such as a circle or square.
words.example: Look at the round shape of the ball.

words.definition: A round shape with no corners.
words.example: Draw a big circle on the paper.

words.definition: A shape with four equal sides and four corners.
A shape with three sides and three corners.
words.example: Cut the paper into a triangle.

words.definition: A shape with four sides and four corners, like a door.
Coming before all others in time or order.
Coming after all others in time or order.
words.example: He was the last one to finish.
words.example_translation: 他是最後一個完成的人。

words.definition: Large in size or amount.
Little in size or amount.
Measuring a great distance from end to end.
Measuring a small distance from end to end.
Weighing a lot; difficult to lift.
Weighing little; easy to lift.
A repeated design or sequence of shapes or colors.
words.example: Can you see the color pattern?
words.example_translation: 你能看到顏色的模式嗎？
